key_insight: The paper introduces a mode-resolved phase-field fatigue model that separates fibre-dominated and matrix/inter-fibre damage, allowing the active fracture mechanism in unidirectional composites to be identified rather than lumped into one damage variable.
engineering_relevance: This gives composite designers a more physically interpretable way to simulate fatigue crack growth, stiffness loss, and orientation-dependent failure across 0°, 45°, and 90° loading cases.
method: Puck-informed split into two fatigue channels: fibre-dominated and matrix/inter-fibre
Each channel uses its own fatigue history, threshold, and fracture-resistance degradation law
Implemented in Abaqus/Standard with a compact UMAT-UEL architecture and two scalar phase fields
evidence_signal: The framework was checked with one-element tests, parameter sweeps, and centred-notch/open-hole tension simulations, reproducing expected crack modes and fatigue-life ordering across orientations and geometries.
watch_item: The study is a numerical verification rather than an experimentally calibrated life-prediction model, so predictive accuracy for new materials or load spectra still needs validation.
paper_title: A Puck-informed mode-resolved phase-field fatigue framework for unidirectional composites
